question: The word 'cake' has a silent E at the end. What vowel sound does the A make — short A (like in 'cat') or long A (like saying the letter name 'A')?
hints: When a word has a vowel-consonant-E pattern, the first vowel usually says its name.
solution: In 'cake', the A makes the long A sound (it says its letter name). The silent E at the end makes the A say its name: c-a-k-e → /k/ /ā/ /k/.

question: Which word has a long vowel sound: 'hop' or 'hope'?
hints: One word has a silent E at the end. That usually makes the vowel say its name.
solution: 'Hope' has a long O sound because of the silent E. 'Hop' has a short O sound. Adding the silent E changes the vowel from short to long.

question: The word 'rain' has the vowel team AI. Does the AI make the long A sound or the long I sound?
hints: In vowel teams, the first vowel usually says its name: A-I → the A says its name.
solution: The vowel team AI in 'rain' makes the long A sound. The rule 'when two vowels go walking, the first one does the talking' applies here: A says its name.

question: Which word uses a vowel team to make a long E sound: 'bed', 'seed', or 'made'?
hints: A vowel team is two vowels together. Look for two vowels side by side.
'Seed' has E-E together in the middle.
solution: 'Seed' uses the vowel team EE, which makes the long E sound. 'Bed' has a short E (no vowel team). 'Made' uses silent E to make a long A.

question: Look at these words: 'coat', 'cone', 'goat', 'note'. Two of them use the silent-E pattern and two use a vowel team. Which two use a vowel team? Type them separated by a comma.
hints: A vowel team is two vowels right next to each other (like OA). Silent-E has a vowel, then a consonant, then E.
Look for the O-A pair inside the words.
solution: 'Coat' and 'goat' use the vowel team OA to make the long O sound. 'Cone' and 'note' use the silent-E pattern (vowel-consonant-E) to make the long O sound. Both patterns make the same long O, but they use different spelling patterns.
